question_text: Which statement about Jupiter and Mars is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Jupiter is closer to the Sun than Mars, so Jupiter has a shorter orbital period

options.label: B
options.text: Mars is closer to the Sun than Jupiter, so Mars has a shorter orbital period

options.label: C
options.text: Both planets have the same orbital period because they orbit the same Sun

options.label: D
options.text: Jupiter has a shorter orbital period because it is larger than Mars

explanation: Mars is closer to the Sun than Jupiter, so Mars orbits faster and has a shorter orbital period (about 2 years) compared to Jupiter (about 12 years). Planet size doesn't affect orbital period—distance from the Sun does.
